solve_notes: Added a `str_starts_with` guard at the top of `stripPrefix` that throws `\InvalidArgumentException` when the path does not begin with the expected prefix. `stripDirectoryPrefix` already delegates to `stripPrefix`, so it gets the validation for free.
task_text:
Fix PathPrefixer.stripPrefix not validating that the path starts with the prefix

In `src/PathPrefixer.php`, `stripPrefix` and `stripDirectoryPrefix`
call `substr($path, strlen($this->prefix))` unconditionally, without
first checking that `$path` actually begins with `$this->prefix`.
When an underlying adapter returns a path that does not start with
the expected prefix (e.g., an absolute path from a misconfigured
storage backend), `stripPrefix` silently returns a garbled
sub-string instead of surfacing an error. Fix `stripPrefix` to use
`str_starts_with` to assert the prefix is present and throw
`\InvalidArgumentException` when it is absent; update
`stripDirectoryPrefix` to rely on the corrected `stripPrefix`.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: prefixPath
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: understanding how prefix is constructed helps confirm that str_starts_with is the right comparison tool and what edge cases to consider

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers the two directly relevant methods. An AI agent would also look at prefixPath to understand how $this->prefix is constructed (especially the separator appended in the constructor) to avoid misunderstanding what string to check with str_starts_with. Without this context, the agent might doubt whether the prefix already includes the separator.
